A Wedding Journey · Bir, Himachal Pradesh, India

Yaroslav&Tseyang

20 · June · 2026
Sukoon Baag Eco Farmstay · Bir Colony · Beneath the Dhauladhar Range

Tibet met Ukraine here. Now they'd like to meet you.

A Word from Us

Welcome to Bir

Bir is a small village tucked into the folds of the lower Himalayas — pine-covered ridges above, a quiet valley below, and the air carrying a hint of wood smoke and something ancient. For most of you, this will be your first time here. For Tseyang, it is her second home.

She arrived here as a child, one of thousands of Tibetans who made the journey across the mountains into India. The colony — its monasteries, its people, the smell of butter lamps — is where she found ground again. That is why we are getting married here.

This guide is your soft landing. What to expect, what to pack, how to get here, what's worth doing. For everything else, there's a WhatsApp group.

The Journey

Getting to Bir

No train reaches Bir — which is part of its charm. You arrive by mountain road. Three options, all well-worn.

Before anything else — at Delhi airport, get a local SIM card (Airtel or Jio, arrivals hall, ₹300–500) and cash in rupees (₹3,000–5,000). Cards (Visa/Mastercard) are accepted in most shops and restaurants, but taxis and public transport are cash-only — so carry some notes.

Your route to Bir · Delhi to the Dhauladhar range
✦ Recommended

A — Fly to Dharamsala, then Taxi

Fly into Dharamsala / Kangra (DHM) — 1 hour from Delhi. Drive to Bir: ~2.5 hours through cedar forests and mountain passes. Pre-arrange your taxi using the trusted driver list in the WhatsApp group — message 24 hours before arrival.

🌙 Overnight

B — Overnight Volvo Bus from Delhi

Departs ISBT Kashmere Gate, New Delhi in the early evening. Arrives Bir by morning — roughly 12 hours. Arrival point: Sangrai Parking, Bir Colony. We will meet you there. Book via RedBus or HRTC. Bring a blanket.

📍 ISBT Delhi on Maps
🚗 Flexible

C — Private Taxi from Delhi

10–12 hours, your own schedule. The scenery after Mandi is spectacular. Approximate cost: ₹10,000–12,000 for the full taxi. Use the trusted driver list in the WhatsApp group.

Your Ground Crew · From Arrival to Departure
Tsewang Has You Covered

Tseyang's brother Tsewang is your single point of contact for everything — from the moment you land to the moment you leave. Transport, logistics, directions, taxi coordination, unexpected situations. Please reach out to him, not to us. We will be a little occupied.

Lost at ISBT? Taxi didn't show? Don't know which road? He's been expecting your call.

Name
Tsewang

Where to Rest

Places to Stay

Book early — Bir is small and June fills up. All options are at the venue or a short auto-rickshaw ride away.

🗓️
Plan to be in Bir the evening of June 19th at the latest. The reception at Sukoon Baag begins at 11:00 on June 20th — make your own way there by taxi or bike. Travel days in India have their own rhythm; give yourself the night before to arrive calmly and rest.
What to Bring & Wear

Pack Once, Cover Everything

June in Bir is early Himalayan summer. Days are warm and clear; nights drop faster than you expect. Brief pre-monsoon showers are possible. Layer, and you're fine.

☀️
Days25–30°C · Warm & sunny
🌙
Nights15–18°C · Jacket mandatory
🌦️
ShowersPossible · Short & dramatic
🎒 Pack This
Light breathable clothes for daytime
Warm fleece or jacket — mandatory
Light rain jacket or compact umbrella
Comfortable walking shoes
Sun cream & sunglasses (altitude UV)
One smart outfit for the ceremony
Phone charged & ready
Small daypack for explorations

Everyone: one palette colour as top or trousers, paired with any light neutral for the other piece. Ladies can also wear a dress in any of these colours instead. These are suggestions — wear what makes you feel yourself.

Bir & Beyond

How Much Time Is Worth Adding?

Bir is not a "tick off 20 sights" kind of place. It has a few very good moods: monasteries, tea gardens, short walks, long views. The question is how much time you have and what kind of day you want.

Half Day
Just an Afternoon in Bir
3–5 hoursLow effortZero planning

Walk the Tibetan Colony, visit a gompa, eat momos, watch paragliders land. A complete half-day — no vehicle, no guide, nothing booked.

  • Tibetan Colony and Chokling Monastery
  • Momos, chai, or butter tea at a colony stall
  • Landing field — paragliders drift in from Billing all afternoon
Worth arriving early for? Yes, for one relaxed afternoon. Not if you expect packed sightseeing.
One Day
Bir + One Easy Excursion
Full dayLow–moderateThe sweet spot

Option A — Culture + scenery: Morning in Bir → Baijnath Temple (800 years old, 40 min away) → tea gardens around Palampur → back by sunset.

Option B — Bir + nature: Morning in the colony → Gunehar waterfall → late afternoon at the landing field or a café.

Worth arriving a day early? Clearly yes — you leave with a real sense of the place.
Two Days
Bir as a Base, Not Just a Stop
2 daysModerateStrongest efficient option

One soft day in the colony. One proper day out — choose one:

  • Baijnath + Palampur — culture and tea-estate scenery
  • Barot Valley — river, forest, proper outdoors
  • Billing / Rajgundha — high altitude, big mountain feeling
Worth buying flights 2 days earlier? Yes. One soft day, one memorable day — the trip feels full.
3–5 Days
A Real Mountain Trip
3–5 daysModerateWedding woven into a larger trip

Now you're not filling time before the wedding — you're having an actual Himachal trip. Combine Bir with Baijnath, Palampur, Barot Valley, and Dharamshala / McLeod Ganj as a bigger side trip or overnight.

Worth coming almost a week early? Yes — for those who like mountain travel and slower days.
Working?
Bir Works for Remote Days Too
FlexibleLow effort if plannedArrive early without full leave

The key isn't random café Wi-Fi — it's choosing the right base with a solid connection. Work blocks in the morning, monastery walk or landing field at sunset. Zero pressure to explore.

Worth arriving early if you still need to work? Yes. Bir is one of the few places where a workation actually makes sense.

🛵
Best for freedom

Rent a scooter or motorbike. Most of the colony and nearby spots are reachable this way. Good for those comfortable on hilly roads.

🚗
Best for comfort / groups

Pre-book a private driver. There's no reliable "just grab a taxi" system here — plan a day ahead. Ask Tsewang for contacts.

Where to Eat in Bir

Bir punches above its size for food. The Tibetan community has built a quiet café culture — wander in, sit down, take your time.

🌸
Day 1 · June 20th, 2026
The Ceremony & Fusion Reception

A blessing in the family shrine room. Ukrainian warmth by evening.

08:00 – 11:00
Family Ceremony — Home Shrine Room Tibetan

In Tibetan tradition, the most sacred ceremonies happen at home — in the family's shrine room, surrounded by the objects and rituals of a lifetime. Expect chanting, the exchange of Khatag scarves — white silk offered as a sign of respect — and moments of quiet reverence. Tseyang will share etiquette guidance beforehand.

By 11:00
Arrive at Sukoon Baag All Guests

Make your own way to the venue by taxi or bike — coordinates and directions are in the WhatsApp group. Please be there by 11:00. The morning ceremony will already have taken place; the reception begins shortly after.

11:30 – 13:30
Greetings & Lunch Reception Together

A joyful daytime gathering — light Indian and Tibetan food, cheerful music, the first proper hellos. No agenda. Just the pleasure of everyone being in the same place.

13:30 – 17:00
Free Time Rest

Rest, wander, nap. The bride and groom will be taking portraits. The mountains will still be there when you wake up.

17:00 – 19:30
Ukrainian Fusion Celebration Ukrainian

🍞 Коровай — a braided ceremonial bread is presented and shared. In Ukrainian tradition, whoever takes the largest piece leads the household. You've been warned.


🎭 Lighter moments (×1–3) — Ukrainian wedding games. Participation strongly encouraged. Dignity: optional.


🕯️ Heartfelt moments (×1–2) — wishes shared, something to carry home. Have a tissue somewhere nearby.

19:30 – 21:00
Dinner, Music & Fire Together

A long, unhurried dinner. Music. Dancing if the moment calls for it. And if the sky holds — a fire. No schedule from here. Stay as long as you like.

🛕
Day 2 · June 21st
Morning at Sherabling, Then Adventure

Monastery at noon together. Then the day is yours.

From 12:00
Sherabling Monastery — Together Tibetan

We start as a group at Sherabling Monastery — one of the most significant Kagyu Buddhist centres outside Tibet, and the place that shaped Tseyang's entire sense of home. Come with no expectations. The chanting, the courtyard, the monks going about their morning — you'll understand why this place matters when you're standing inside it.

📍 Sherabling Monastery
Afternoon Onwards
Choose Your Adventure Your Choice

After the monastery, the rest of the day is yours.

🪂
Tandem Paragliding

A tandem flight from Billing — one of the world's premier launch points. We're covering the cost of this experience. Contact the instructor directly to book your slot: [Instructor number — TBC]

On Us · Billing Takeoff
🏛️
Dharamsala Day Trip

McLeod Ganj — the Dalai Lama's home in exile. Tibetan temples, prayer wheels, excellent coffee. Group trip can be organised.

~3 hrs each way
⛺
Mountain Camping

Overnight into the high ground above Bir for those who signed up. Stars, silence, altitude.

Pre-signup required
India Wonders · For the Curious & the Restless
You're Already Here.
Go Further.

India is not a country you visit once and understand. But here's a start — places worth the detour, decisions made simple.

The Classic Circuit

The Golden Triangle

Three cities, all within reach of Delhi. Each gives you something entirely different.

🕌
Delhi
Day 1–2

Old Delhi's lanes, the Red Fort, Humayun's Tomb, the best street food in the country. Overwhelming — then addictive.

1–2 days · Entry free–₹500
🏯
Agra
Day 3

The Taj Mahal actually exceeds its reputation. Go at sunrise. Agra Fort is excellent and half as crowded.

1 day · ₹1,100 · 3–4h from Delhi
🎨
Jaipur
Day 4–5

Amber Fort, block-print textiles, and the best dal baati you'll eat. Warmer, more colourful, slightly slower.

1–2 days · 5h from Delhi by train
Your Time
Our Suggestion
2–3 days
Delhi only. Old Delhi alone fills a full day.
4–5 days
Delhi + Agra. Gatimaan Express, 90 min. A solid long weekend.
6–7 days
Full Triangle: Delhi → Agra → Jaipur → Delhi. Book trains on IRCTC in advance.
8+ days
Add Varanasi — the most ancient city on earth. Nothing prepares you for it.
Book trains early — use IRCTC.co.in or a local travel agent in Delhi (small fee, saves real confusion).
Eating in India

A Simple Food Guide

Start conservative, graduate to adventurous. "Medium" spice in India is not what you expect — always ask for mild first.

✓ Start with These
🫓Roti / Chapati — fresh flatbread, tear and scoop. The base of everything.
🍚Chicken Biryani — slow-cooked, aromatic, deeply satisfying.
🍗Afghan Chicken — yoghurt-marinated, mild, great entry point.
🫘Dal Tadka — yellow lentils with ghee. Vegetarian comfort food everywhere.
🥘Paneer Butter Masala — the universally loved vegetarian dish.
⚠ Navigate with Care
🚰Tap water — never directly. Always sealed bottles. Street ice too.
🥗Raw salads / cut fruit from stalls — washed in tap water. Give your gut a few days.
🌶️Unplanned spice — escalate slowly. Your stomach needs a day to adjust.
🍖Pork & beef — rarely available in Buddhist and Hindu areas like Bir.
Golden rule: eat where locals eat in numbers. A crowded dhaba with plastic chairs beats an empty tourist restaurant every time.
༄
With love, from the mountains.

Yaroslav & Tseyang · Bir, Himachal Pradesh · June 2026
